notes: I tried USE_WINED3D, NO_D3D11 and NO_ESYNC but the game don't start or crash immediately.
title: BioShock® 2

notes:
To get past "Press Space to Continue" one needs to run the game with -nointro - that is, in game properties, set Launch Options: "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro". For whatever reason, -nointro makes the game accept input after loading screen!

Presumably the game has similar texture issues with esync as Bioshock (1), but haven't tried without disabling ESYNC.

Seems to run fine, mostly. There are some stuttering / hiccups when transitioning from an area to another; seems the game is loading something. They do not hinder gameplay (as these do not happen mid-action) and might occur even on Windows - have not and can not try!

I've got graphical glitches at one point in Atlantic Express after Lamb talks you and you fall into water. Haven't played trough Atlantic Express yet.
title: BioShock® 2

notes:
Out of the box experience... 
The game crash in DX10 mode, but that is a Proton/DXVK issue. 
In DX9 mode the game works perfectly. 
It is necessary to add this to the game launch options for a proper experience:  PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -dx9

I played the first TWO levels in a row without any single crash.
title: BioShock® 2

notes:
Brightness slider not working, but game is playable, 40-60 FPS;    


-- Multiplayer: I  Tested "Private Mod" , but FPS constantly drops to 1 - 10, so it is not playable.
title: BioShock® 2

notes: Used "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -dx9 -nointro" argument and the game loaded and run perfectly for the first chapter. However upon reaching the load screen of the second chapter the game crashed.
title: BioShock® 2

notes: Install the game with protonfixes wont need any more tweaks. It runs perfect and stable

title: BioShock® 2

notes: The game runs perfectly. If you don't disable Esync then all the surface textures will be very low resolution and blurry. Also, when loading each map, the game asks you to press spacebar but doesn't respond to any keys. As a workaround I have to plug in a DS4 controller and push the A (X) button to progress. Then when the map loads I unplug the controller and press the spacebar to change control schemes. Every other control option works fine, tab is perminately mapped to a terminal command however.
title: BioShock® 2
